item.title: Sightings 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison describes building a 'Sightings' feature on his blog to syndicate wildlife photos from iNaturalist, using a new Canon R6 Mark II camera. He extended his existing 'beats' system using Claude Code for web on his phone, back-populating over a decade of iNaturalist sightings. The sightings appear on his homepage, date archive pages, and site search results, allowing users to find photos like his 2019 lemur images from Madagascar.
标签: blogging, photography, wildlife, AI, iNaturalist, generative-ai, LLMs, AI-assisted programming, Claude Code, syndication

item.title: How backups work depends on the goals of the people setting them up 摘要
item.description:
A SaaS provider's volume-level backups, stored in the same volume as the data, were deleted along with the production database when the volume was wiped. This design protects against accidental data loss (e.g., DROP TABLE) but not deliberate volume deletion. The provider likely made a tradeoff: making backups hard to delete would annoy customers who want to avoid charges for unused volumes. External backups are recommended for catastrophic recovery, but they are slower and costlier. The incident highlights the importance of understanding backup recovery goals and customer needs.
标签: backup, SaaS, database, disaster recovery, ZFS, cloud, data loss, tradeoffs, volume deletion, incident
